name: setup
description: Install QMD and configure collections for local hybrid search (BM25 + vector + re-ranking). Run this first after installing the skill. Creates collections based on workspace structure: workspace (core agent files), daily-logs, intelligence, and projects directories. Do not run this again if collections already exist - use refresh to update the index instead.

name: calculate_savings
description: Calculate monthly and annual cost savings by switching from cloud-based memory APIs to QMD local search. Prompts for daily search frequency and average cost per API call, then outputs estimated savings. Use this to understand the ROI of local search. This is a read-only calculation tool - it does not modify any settings.

searches_per_day.description: Estimated number of memory searches per day. Typical range: 20-200.

cost_per_call.description: Average cost per API call in dollars. Typical range: $0.01-$0.08 depending on provider and operation type.

name: refresh
description: Rebuild QMD search index and regenerate all vector embeddings. Use this after adding, modifying, or deleting files in your workspace. Runs qmd update (rebuilds BM25 index) followed by qmd embed (regenerates vector embeddings). Do not use this for single file changes - it regenerates the entire index. For scheduled updates, a cron job is set up automatically during setup.

name: serve
description: Start QMD as a shared MCP server for multi-agent memory sharing. Enables multiple agents (Forge, Thoth, Axis) to query a collective knowledge base. Returns server status and connection info. Do not use this for single-agent setups - the local CLI works without a server. Only run once - subsequent calls return the running server status.

name: add_collection
description: Add a new directory as a searchable collection in the QMD index. Collections group related documents for targeted searching. Do not add collections that are already indexed (workspace, daily-logs, intelligence, projects are created by setup).

path.description: Absolute path to the directory to add as a collection. Must exist and contain files matching the mask.

name.description: Short name for the collection used in queries (e.g., 'research', 'projects', 'notes'). Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.

mask.description: Glob pattern for files to include. Common values: '**/*.md' (all markdown), '*.txt' (root level text files), '**/*.txt' (all text files).

name: add_context
description: Add a natural language description to an existing collection to improve search relevance. Context helps QMD's hybrid search understand what type of content is in each collection. Use this after adding new collections to improve result quality.

collection.description: Collection name to add context to. Use the collection name without the qmd:// prefix (e.g., 'research', 'workspace', 'daily-logs').

context.description: Natural language description of what this collection contains. Be specific: 'Market research, competitive analysis, and industry reports from 2024-2025' rather than 'research documents'.

name: apply_template
description: Apply a pre-configured workspace template to quickly set up collections for a specific use case. Templates create multiple collections optimized for different workflows. Do not use this if you already have custom collections - it may create conflicting collections.

template.description: Template name: 'trading' creates intelligence, market-data, research, daily-logs for investors; 'content' creates articles, drafts, research, ideas for creators; 'developer' creates docs, notes, decisions, snippets for engineers.

name: qmd_search
description: Fast keyword-only search using BM25 algorithm. Use this when you need exact term matches, proper nouns, or code snippets. Do not use this for conceptual queries - it only matches exact words. This is the fastest search mode but has lowest recall for natural language questions. For conceptual similarity use qmd_vsearch. For best accuracy use qmd_query.

query.description: Search query with exact terms to match. Use quotes for phrases: 'Charlene Smith', 'API key'. Do not use natural language questions.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Range: 1-100.

collection.description: Optional collection name to restrict search to. If not provided, searches all collections. Use collection name without qmd:// prefix.

name: qmd_vsearch
description: Semantic vector search for conceptual similarity. Use this when you need to find content related to an idea even if exact terms don't match, e.g., 'how should we handle customer complaints', 'what decisions about pricing'. Do not use this when you need exact matches - it only finds conceptually similar content. Slower than keyword search but understands meaning. For exact matches use qmd_search. For best accuracy use qmd_query.

query.description: Search query as a natural language concept or question. Can be a phrase: 'how to optimize database queries', 'best practices for error handling'. The search finds conceptually related content.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Range: 1-100.

name: qmd_query
description: Hybrid search with query expansion and LLM re-ranking. This is the recommended default search mode - it combines BM25 keyword matching, semantic vector search, expands the query with synonyms, and re-ranks results using a local LLM. Use this for complex queries, important research, or when you need the most accurate results. Do not use this for simple exact-match lookups where speed matters - qmd_search is faster.

query.description: Search query in natural language. Can be a question: 'what decisions did we make about pricing strategy', 'how do we handle API authentication'. The query expansion will find relevant content even with imperfect matches.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return after re-ranking. Range: 1-100. Higher limits return more results but take longer.

collection.description: Optional collection name to restrict search to. If not provided, searches all collections. Use collection name without qmd:// prefix.
